title: Examination of Carrick

sections.body:
She was going to give me the file in the morning. I had every reason to want her alive. She told me she suspected someone was quietly modifying old base archive logs from an elevated network terminal, but she hadn't pinned down the exact account yet.

Trust this sheet if thou wilt; the sergeant trusted it not, yet kept it. One of the detained souls lies outright, another lies by omission, and a third speaketh true in so crooked a manner that truth itself seems perjured.

A prudent reader will note what is said, and more keenly what is artfully left unsaid. The desk-note beneath it read only: “keep apart from the louder papers.”

subtitle: Marked reliable by the clerk who was himself twice found mistaken.
